action: Implement a real same-host atomic compare-and-set adapter and verify conflicting terminals across independent OS processes.

progress.finding: A shared filesystem can provide cross-process terminal uniqueness when compare-and-set is serialized by exclusive lock-directory creation and the expected digest is revalidated inside the critical section.
progress.verification: 1 Node test passed in an isolated runtime: two independent processes produced one recorded terminal, one terminal-conflict, and one persisted terminal on fresh read.

handoff: Wire this adapter into a real peer-runtime caller, add crash-injection tests around write, sync, rename, and lock release, then choose a distributed conditional-write backend for multi-host execution.
